Deep security audit: header injection, request smuggling, HEAD suppression, keep-alive, Expect 100-continue, error handling — all SOUND
Deep security audit: header validation, Content-Length vs Transfer-Encoding, encoding — all SOUND

Deep security audit: SCRAM-SHA-256 constant-time OK, no SQL injection (Extended Query Protocol), TLS downgrade only in Prefer mode (by design), wire parsing bounds-checked, cancel-in-flight properly closes connection

MEDIUM: HPACK encoder table size not capped — peer SETTINGS could request unbounded encoder table growth. Capped to 1 MiB matching decoder limit. Also deep security audit: stream IDs, flow control, GOAWAY, settings, priority — all SOUND.
Deep security audit: certificate verification, TLS version negotiation, SNI, key material cleanup, handshake state machine — all SOUND, delegated to rustls correctly
Deep audit: in-flight tracking (RAII + checked_sub), SWRR weights, P2C, health checks — all SOUND
Deep concurrency audit: P2C selection, circular_index, lock ordering — all SOUND. SKIPPED_LOCALS_INLINE_CAP=8 is intentional scan limit, not a bug.
Deep concurrency audit: steal/steal_batch with arena lock, FIFO/LIFO, is_local check-to-use — all SOUND under arena lock
Deep re-audit: obligation leak detection, token validation, abort_by_id, reset safety — all SOUND. ArenaIndex field order is cosmetic (previously noted batch 328).

Fresh-eyes audit: ChildStdin::poll_shutdown falsely reported success without closing stdin EOF; Command::env_remove failed to suppress inherited environment variables. Added focused regressions for both.

Fresh-eyes audit: TcpSocket::bind() silently overwrote an existing local bind, causing last-writer-wins identity for later listen/connect. Rejected rebinds and added a focused regression preserving the original bind.
Fresh-eyes audit: holder secondary index now preserves multiple TaskId generations sharing one arena slot instead of hiding older-generation obligation IDs.
Fresh-eyes audit: task/region secondary indexes now preserve same-slot generation reuse instead of clearing older symbolic obligation lookups.

WorkerCoordinator::request_shutdown() used to leave queued SpawnJob/PollStatus/CancelJob envelopes ahead of ShutdownWorker. Shutdown now discards stale outbound job traffic and focused regressions lock the boundary down.
GaussianSolver no longer reports Solved for underdetermined rectangular systems and no longer returns row-shaped RHS data for solved overdetermined systems. Added focused 3x2 and 2x3 regressions.
Fresh-eyes audit: IoOp silently leaked reserved obligations on handle drop. Added fail-closed drop guard, explicit into_raw escape hatch, and focused regressions.

Fresh-eyes re-audit: Command env/env_remove bookkeeping diverged from std::process::Command on Windows because builder keys were case-sensitive. Reworked env changes onto case-preserving EnvKey semantics and added focused Windows regressions.

Fresh-eyes re-audit: force_abort_repair rewrote broken entries without any monotonic repair marker, so stale merges could resurrect conflicts or linearity violations. Added convergent repair_nodes metadata plus deterministic repaired-tombstone normalization and focused merge-stability regressions.

Fresh-eyes re-audit: recovery tick auto-resolve depended on non-merge-stable CRDT repairs. Added end-to-end regression proving conflict repair remains aborted and linear after a stale conflicting merge.
Fresh-eyes follow-up: force_abort_repair created a ghost Unknown entry when called on a missing obligation ID. Missing-ID repair is now a true no-op, with a focused regression.

Fresh-eyes follow-up: Windows EnvKey updates kept stale key casing because BTreeMap::insert preserved the old equal key. env/envs/env_remove now replace equal-key entries before reinserting, and focused latest-casing regressions were added.

Fresh-eyes audit: update_from_discover consumed only delta batches and returned early on empty changes, so late-joining or shared-consumer balancers could stay permanently empty despite a live discovery snapshot. Reconciled topology against discover.endpoints() after applying deltas and added a focused late-join regression.

Fresh-eyes review of fellow-agent session-reset changes: only bootstrap operations carried worker identity, so after a worker reboot a stale higher-sequence message from the replaced worker could be accepted as fresh, poison last_inbound_seq_no, and block the new session. Added explicit worker-session identity to worker-originated envelopes, rejected mismatched inbound sessions, updated the canonical worker harness, and added focused regressions.
verify_integrity ignored schema_version tampering; hash now covers schema_version and regression added
Self-links were double-indexed in task_index, duplicating peer views and resolve_exits() actions.
poll ignored wake/next-deadline semantics; chaos delay rebased from poll time; closed-fault HUP was lost on idle polls; injected close survived clear_fault_config
Latched timer/fallback wake readiness so fired Sleep cannot be re-armed when later polled under a different earlier clock
TimerDriver::update now fails closed when asked to update a stale or cancelled handle instead of silently registering a new timer
Registration::deregister kept Drop armed on persistent explicit deregister error/panic so consumed handles still get a final best-effort cleanup pass; added direct drop-cleanup regression
DnsCache now stores negative no-record entries using negative_ttl and serves them case-insensitively until expiry instead of ignoring CacheConfig::negative_ttl.
Resolver now fail-closes no-record lookup errors into cached NoRecords results, skips pointless retries for them, and classifies NXDOMAIN-like std::net errors for negative caching.
Deep audit: atomic state machine (u64 bit-packing CAS), HalfOpen probe logic with epoch-based stale prevention, sliding window, metrics consistency, cooldown/recovery — all SOUND

Deep audit: retry budget (value-based policy chain correct), no backoff overflow (immediate retries), cancel-clean drop, idempotency delegated to Policy trait, cooperative budget prevents monopolization
Deep audit: protocol enforcement via move semantics, ObligationToken drop guard with double-panic suppression, cancel-correctness, no deadlock risk — all SOUND

Deep re-audit: double-execution prevention (WakeState + lock), waker correctness (finish_poll re-schedule), cancel propagation (inject_cancel + cancel lane), Tarjan SCC deadlock detection, EXP3 adaptive (latent f64 overflow after ~700 epochs, degrades benignly) — all SOUND
